commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A maintainer should review the hook-context shape and decide whether to require live restart proof before merge; there is no narrow ClawSweeper code repair to queue.; Cleared: Only agent delivery code and a focused test changed; no dependency, workflow, secret, or supply-chain surface was introduced. (sha=445bb4a603dff889d0587717e7cd3e50dfa8b932)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `reply_payload_sending` hook metadata to agent-command durable final deliveries and adds focused regression coverage for the outbound delivery arguments.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +51. Total +64 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main omits `replyPayloadSendingHook` on the agent-command durable final delivery call, while outbound delivery only runs `reply_payload_sending` when that metadata is present. I did not run a live gateway restart reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should review the hook-context shape and decide whether to require live restart proof before merge; there is no narrow ClawSweeper code repair to queue.

target.title: fix: route agent command replies through reply hooks
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes agent-command durable final delivery so recovered/main-session replies carry `reply_payload_sending` hook metadata into the existing outbound send path.
- This lets channel plugins that rely on `reply_payload_sending` observe, rewrite, cancel, or fallback-deliver recovered agent replies instead of missing them.

Why does this matter now?

- #90282 reports message loss after gateway restart recovery: the agent produces the reply, but plugin fallback delivery hooks never run.

What is the intended outcome?

- Agent-command final deliveries now use the same replayable reply-payload hook contract already supported by durable outbound delivery.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No new recovery event, plugin API, session schema, or delivery queue schema.

What does success look like?

- Durable final delivery receives `kind: "final"`, channel, session key, run id, and message context for `reply_payload_sending`.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the hook metadata shape matches the existing routed reply/durable outbound contract and remains safe for queue replay.

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90282

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90282

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; issue was labeled `clawsweeper:queueable-fix` and `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`.

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: restart-recovered agent-command final replies now pass replayable `reply_payload_sending` hook metadata into durable outbound delivery.
- Real environment tested: Windows local OpenClaw checkout; source-level regression coverage for the durable final delivery seam.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config test\vitest\vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 21 passed (21)`.
- Observed result after fix: the regression test observes `replyPayloadSendingHook` with `kind: "final"`, channel, session key, run id, and context on the durable delivery call.
- What was not tested: a live gateway restart with a real channel plugin.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no live channel credentials or restart harness were used in this local run; proof is focused on the missing source-level delivery seam identified in #90282.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the new regression test first failed with `expected undefined to deeply equal { kind: 'final', ... }` before the implementation change.

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config test\vitest\vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check src\agents\command\delivery.ts src\agents\command\delivery.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/command/delivery.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts`
- `npx -y pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core:test`
- `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `deliverAgentCommandResult` coverage asserting durable final delivery receives replayable `reply_payload_sending` hook metadata.
- Made two existing workspace-dir assertions platform-neutral with `path.resolve(...)` so the focused file passes on Windows too.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The new regression test failed before the implementation because `replyPayloadSendingHook` was `undefined` on durable delivery args.

If no test was added, why not?

- A regression test was added.

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Recovered/main-session agent-command final replies can now run the existing `reply_payload_sending` hook path before delivery.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Plugin hooks now run for this agent-command durable final delivery path, matching the existing reply delivery contract.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change uses the already-supported durable outbound hook field and keeps queue/replay behavior in the existing outbound delivery layer.
- Focused regression coverage checks the metadata passed to durable delivery.

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- A live gateway restart/channel-plugin proof would be useful if maintainers want end-to-end transport validation beyond the source-level seam test.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local autoreview reported clean: no accepted/actionable findings.

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>
